# Email Channel

## Overview

Email channel ‚Äî IMAP IDLE listener + SMTP sender backed by the ``GSageEmailAccount`` table.

The Email worker runs in the ``email_worker`` container. It polls / IDLE-watches every active ``GSageEmailAccount`` row and dispatches incoming messages to MCP after rate limiting and thread resolution. Outbound replies are sent via SMTP using the same account credentials. Each organisation can register multiple mailboxes; messages from unknown senders are moved into the ``email_unknown_sender_folder`` (default ``Unknown-Senders``).

## Quick facts

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Interface key | `email` |
| Config storage | `email_account_table` |
| CLI module | `src.ops_cli.channels.email` |
| Worker modules | `src.email_worker.main` |

## Per-organisation configuration

> The JSON below describes the per-organisation configuration. Each entry corresponds to one row in the `GSageEmailAccount` table; the columns map 1:1 to the fields below.

> **Sensitive fields** (passwords, tokens, secrets) are encrypted at rest by the storage backend, but the JSON below shows the logical shape ‚Äî supply real values through the CLI's `--*-stdin` flags.

### Empty template (required fields only)

```json
{
  "display_name": "<value>",
  "email": "<value>",
  "imap_host": "<value>",
  "imap_port": 993,
  "imap_username": "<value>",
  "imap_password": "<value>",
  "smtp_host": "<value>",
  "smtp_port": 587
}
```

### Example payload

```json
{
  "display_name": "<value>",
  "email": "<value>",
  "imap_folder": "INBOX",
  "imap_host": "<value>",
  "imap_password": "<value>",
  "imap_port": 993,
  "imap_use_tls": true,
  "imap_username": "<value>",
  "imap_verify_ssl": true,
  "max_email_size_bytes": 0,
  "polling_interval_seconds": 0,
  "sender_name": "<value>",
  "smtp_host": "<value>",
  "smtp_password": "<value>",
  "smtp_port": 587,
  "smtp_use_tls": true,
  "smtp_username": "",
  "smtp_verify_ssl": true,
  "subject_prefix": "<value>"
}
```

### Field reference

| Field | Type | Required | Sensitive | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | :---: | :---: | --- | --- |
| `display_name` | `string` | ‚úì | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | Human-readable label (e.g. ``SOC Mailbox``). |
| `email` | `string` | ‚úì | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | From address; also the unique key for the account row. |
| `imap_folder` | `string` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | `"INBOX"` | Mailbox to watch. |
| `imap_host` | `string` | ‚úì | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | IMAP server hostname. |
| `imap_password` | `string` | ‚úì | ‚úì | ‚Äî | IMAP password (encrypted at rest). |
| `imap_port` | `integer` | ‚úì | ‚Äî | `993` | IMAP port (993 for IMAPS, 143 for STARTTLS). |
| `imap_use_tls` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | `true` | Use IMAPS / STARTTLS. |
| `imap_username` | `string` | ‚úì | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | IMAP login. |
| `imap_verify_ssl` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | `true` | Verify TLS certificates (set to ``false`` for self-signed). |
| `max_email_size_bytes` | `integer` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | Reject inbound messages larger than this. |
| `polling_interval_seconds` | `integer` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | Fallback poll cadence when IMAP IDLE is not available. |
| `sender_name` | `string` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | Display name used in the ``From:`` header. |
| `smtp_host` | `string` | ‚úì | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | SMTP server hostname. |
| `smtp_password` | `string` | ‚Äî | ‚úì | ‚Äî | SMTP password (encrypted at rest). |
| `smtp_port` | `integer` | ‚úì | ‚Äî | `587` | SMTP port (587 for STARTTLS, 465 for SMTPS). |
| `smtp_use_tls` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | `true` | Use STARTTLS / SMTPS. |
| `smtp_username` | `string` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | `""` | SMTP login. Leave blank for unauthenticated relay. |
| `smtp_verify_ssl` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | `true` | Verify TLS certificates. |
| `subject_prefix` | `string` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | Optional subject prefix for outbound messages. |


## Environment variables

These cluster-wide variables seed the channel's runtime defaults. Set them in your `.env` (or via your secrets backend) before starting the worker. **Per-org configuration** (see the previous section) takes precedence at runtime.

| Variable | Type | Default | Sensitive | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
| `EMAIL_DELETE_AFTER_PROCESS` | `boolean` | `false` | ‚Äî | When ``true`` permanently delete messages after processing; otherwise they are only marked ``\Seen``. |
| `EMAIL_RATE_LIMIT_ORG_DAILY` | `integer` | `100` | ‚Äî | Maximum inbound messages processed per organisation per UTC day. |
| `EMAIL_RATE_LIMIT_USER_HOURLY` | `integer` | `10` | ‚Äî | Maximum new threads opened per user per rolling hour. |
| `EMAIL_UNKNOWN_SENDER_FOLDER` | `string` | `Unknown-Senders` | ‚Äî | IMAP folder where messages from unknown senders are moved (auto-created on first use). |
| `SMTP_DEFAULT_FORMAT` | `string` | `html` | ‚Äî | Default body format for outbound notifications: ``html`` (auto-converts Markdown) or ``text``. |
| `SMTP_FROM_EMAIL` | `string` | `noreply@gsage.local` | ‚Äî | Fallback From address for outbound SOC notifications that are not tied to a specific account. |
| `SMTP_FROM_NAME` | `string` | `gSage AI` | ‚Äî | Display name paired with ``SMTP_FROM_EMAIL``. |

## CLI usage

**Create / update an email account (passwords on stdin)**

```bash
printf '%s\n%s\n' '<imap-pw>' '<smtp-pw>' | \
    python -m ops_cli channels email create \
        --org-slug gsage \
        --display-name "SOC Mailbox" \
        --email soc@example.com \
        --imap-host mail.example.com --imap-port 993 --imap-user soc \
        --smtp-host mail.example.com --smtp-port 587 --smtp-user soc \
        --imap-password-stdin --smtp-password-stdin \
        --test
```

**List accounts in an org**

```bash
python -m ops_cli channels email list --org-slug gsage
```


## Prerequisites

- An IMAP+SMTP-capable mailbox the worker can authenticate to.
- Network egress from `email_worker` to the IMAP and SMTP servers.
- The `email_worker` service must be running (`docker compose up email_worker`).
- Mailboxes are encrypted at rest using the application secret ‚Äî rotating that secret invalidates stored credentials.
